narrative: On Tuesday, September 2, 2025 at 6:30am, Resident Assistant Aisha Okafor was approached by resident Leila Singh of Apartment 39A in Thomas Suites, who asked to speak privately. Aisha met with Leila in the RA office. Leila disclosed that a former partner had been sending repeated unsolicited and sexually explicit messages over the past several weeks. Leila appeared visibly distressed throughout the conversation. Aisha informed Leila that Aisha was a mandated reporter and explained what that meant and what would happen next. Aisha provided Leila with information about VPVPA, RUPD, CAPS, and the confidential sexual violence support advocate. Leila agreed to speak with RUPD. Aisha called RUPD at 6:39am and notified ARLC Michelle Park at 6:39am. ARLC Michelle arrived at the scene at 6:56am. RUPD arrived at 6:56am and conducted a private interview with Leila. RUPD issued CAD number 26-33028. RLC Priya Sharma was notified at 6:56am and arrived at 7:09am to provide additional support and ensure proper protocol was followed. Sharma coordinated next steps with RUPD and ARLC Michelle. RUPD departed at 7:09am. ARLC Michelle remained with Leila to ensure Leila had support and was aware of all available resources. Priya Sharma completed documentation at 7:36am. End of report.

On Wednesday, September 3, 2025 at 3:15pm, Resident Assistant Aisha Okafor responded to a fire alarm activation in Crosby Suites. Aisha evacuated all residents from the floor and conducted a sweep to ensure no one remained. Upon evacuation, Aisha observed visible smoke coming from Apartment 4C. Aisha called RUPD and the fire department at 3:23pm and kept all residents away from the building entrance. The fire department arrived at 3:47pm and identified the source as an overheated power strip that had begun to smolder inside Apartment 4C. The smoldering was extinguished and the power strip was removed. The fire department cleared the building for reentry after ventilating the affected room. No residents were injured. RUPD issued CAD number 26-25081. Aisha completed documentation and submitted the report. End of report.

On Saturday, September 6, 2025 at 2:45am, Resident Assistant Tariq Osei received 5 noise complaints from residents regarding repeated loud banging in Apartment 20D in Judson Suites. Tariq responded and asked the group to reduce noise. The group complied without incident. Tariq confirmed resolution with complaining residents. Tariq notified ARLC Michelle Park of the situation at 3:01am. Documentation was completed at 3:29am. End of report.

Sharma coordinated next steps with RUPD and ARLC Keisha. RUPD departed at 2:10am. ARLC Keisha remained with Zara to ensure Zara had support and was aware of all available resources. On Tuesday, September 9, 2025 at 7:15pm, Resident Assistant Tariq Osei was contacted by resident Sofia Adeyemi reporting that another resident had left laundry sitting in the machines in Common Area of Katzenbach Hall overnight. Tariq posted a 30-minute notice on the machines and moved the unclaimed laundry to the folding table after no one came to claim it. Tariq notified ARLC Keisha Williams of the situation at 7:36pm. Documentation was completed at 7:55pm. End of report.

On Wednesday, September 10, 2025 at 1:30am, Resident Assistant Devon Wallace was informed by resident Rafael Vance that a group had been monopolizing the shared Laundry Room in New Gibbons for an extended period, preventing other residents from using the space. Devon spoke with the group and established equitable usage expectations going forward. Devon notified ARLC Eric Donnelly of the situation at 1:42am. Documentation was completed at 2:15am. End of report.

On Thursday, September 11, 2025 at 5:45am, Resident Assistant Tariq Osei received a complaint about the condition of the communal bathroom in Henderson Apartments. Tariq inspected the space, placed a reminder note, and sent a floor-wide email outlining shared bathroom etiquette expectations. Tariq also coordinated with custodial staff for increased monitoring. Documentation was completed at 6:29am. End of report.

On Friday, September 12, 2025 at 12:00pm, Resident Assistant Aisha Okafor detected the smell of alcohol while conducting rounds on the 3rd floor of Newell Apartments. The smell was concentrated near Apartment 26C. Aisha knocked and identified Aishaself. Resident Janelle Ibarra answered. Aisha observed a case of beer on the floor with open cans. Aisha requested identification from all 4 individuals present: Janelle Ibarra, Chloe Huang, Tomas Petrov, Tiffany Williams. Upon review, all 4 individuals were confirmed to be under 21 years of age, in violation of the university's Alcohol Policy. Aisha disposed of all open and remaining alcohol per policy and verbally notified each individual of the violation. Aisha informed all residents that formal incident reports would be filed for each individual. All cooperated. Aisha contacted ARLC Keisha Williams at 12:19pm. Keisha confirmed next steps. Documentation was completed at 12:28pm. End of report.

On Saturday, September 13, 2025 at 9:00pm, Resident Assistant Devon Wallace observed that the main entrance door of McCormick Suites had been propped open. Devon secured the door and identified resident Elijah Okonkwo as responsible. Devon explained the security implications and reminded Elijah of the guest sign-in procedure. Devon notified ARLC David Ortega of the situation at 9:23pm. Documentation was completed at 9:41pm. End of report.

On Friday, September 19, 2025 at 5:15pm, Resident Assistant Devon Wallace received 5 noise complaints from residents regarding shouting in the hallway in Apartment 37A in Silvers Apartments. Devon responded and asked the group to reduce noise. The group complied without incident. Devon confirmed resolution with complaining residents. Devon notified ARLC Michelle Park of the situation at 5:36pm. Documentation was completed at 5:43pm. End of report.

On Saturday, September 20, 2025 at 9:45pm, Resident Assistant Marcus Thompson was conducting rounds in Campbell Hall when Marcus detected a strong odor of marijuana near Room 709. Marcus knocked on the door and identified Marcusself as the RA on duty. Resident Sandra Farooq answered after approximately one minute. Upon opening the door, the smell of marijuana intensified significantly. Marcus observed a grinder, rolling papers, and a jar of cannabis. Sandra acknowledged using cannabis in the room. Marcus informed Sandra of the university's Drug Policy, noting that cannabis is prohibited on all Rutgers property regardless of age or New Jersey state law, as the university must comply with federal law as a condition of receiving federal funding. Marcus confiscated and disposed of the items per policy and informed Sandra that a formal incident report would be filed with a referral to the Office of Student Conduct and the Alcohol and Other Drug Assistance Program. Sandra cooperated throughout. Marcus contacted ARLC Sarah Nguyen at 10:00pm and received confirmation of next steps. Documentation was completed at 10:11pm. End of report.

On Sunday, September 21, 2025 at 12:30am, Resident Assistant Aisha Okafor was contacted by resident Ingrid Ibarra who reported extensive mold growth covering a large portion of the wall in Room 820 in Quad III and stated that Ingrid had been experiencing persistent respiratory symptoms including coughing and difficulty breathing for the past week. Aisha responded and confirmed significant mold growth spanning approximately three feet along the wall near the window frame. Aisha immediately submitted an urgent health and safety maintenance request and emailed the RD at 12:38am. Facilities responded and assessed the mold as requiring professional remediation. Ingrid was temporarily relocated to a vacant room per RD approval while remediation was completed. Ingrid was also advised to seek medical evaluation for the reported symptoms. Remediation was completed and Ingrid returned to the room. Aisha completed documentation and submitted the report. End of report.

On Sunday, September 21, 2025 at 9:15pm, Resident Assistant Marcus Thompson received multiple complaints from residents on the same floor of Sojourner Truth Apartments that the heating system had completely failed and that room temperatures had dropped to an unsafe level. Marcus responded and confirmed that Suite 713 and adjacent rooms were extremely cold, with indoor temperatures estimated well below a comfortable threshold for sleeping. Marcus called emergency maintenance at 9:20pm and notified the RD immediately. Marcus distributed extra blankets from the RA supply closet to affected residents. Emergency maintenance arrived at 10:06pm and identified a failed heating unit relay affecting the entire floor. A temporary repair was completed by 10:06pm with a full component replacement scheduled. The RD authorized temporary room reassignments for any residents who did not feel the heat was adequate overnight. Marcus completed documentation and submitted the report. End of report.

Emergency maintenance arrived at 3:09pm and began remediation. A plumber was called to address the main line blockage. The bathroom remained out of service for the remainder of the night. Tariq completed documentation and submitted the report. End of report.

The electrician advised Simone to send a floor communication about electrical load limits. Simone completed documentation and submitted the report. End of report.

Upon opening the door, the smell of marijuana intensified significantly. Aisha observed rolling papers, a lighter, and cannabis on the nightstand. Tobias acknowledged using cannabis in the room.
